title: The Rise of Online Learning
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The rise of online (0) education has transformed how people gain knowledge and qualifications. In the past, classroom teaching was the only option, but today there are many (17) traditional and digital alternatives available to learners. Online courses are highly (18) accessible to anyone with an internet connection, removing geographical barriers. However, one major (19) disadvantage is that students may feel isolated without face-to-face contact with peers. Studying from home requires great (20) flexibility, as learners must organise their own schedules. Without strong (21) motivation, many students struggle to complete online programmes successfully. The lack of direct (22) interaction with teachers can make it harder to ask questions and get immediate support. An (23) inability to stay focused is another common challenge reported by online learners. Despite these issues, many students find that their (24) achievements through digital learning are just as impressive as those gained in traditional classrooms.

example.text: The rise of online (0) EDUCATION has transformed how people gain knowledge and qualifications. (EDUCATE)

questions.explanation: An adjective is needed before 'and digital'; 'tradition' becomes 'traditional' by adding the suffix '-al'.
An adjective is needed after 'highly'; 'access' becomes 'accessible' by adding the suffix '-ible'.
A negative noun is needed; the prefix 'dis-' is added to 'advantage' to mean a drawback or negative aspect.
A noun is needed after 'great'; 'flexible' becomes 'flexibility' by changing '-ible' to '-ibility'.
A noun is needed after 'strong'; 'motivate' becomes 'motivation' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
A noun is needed after 'direct'; 'interact' becomes 'interaction' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
A negative noun is needed; 'able' → 'ability', then the prefix 'in-' creates 'inability', meaning a lack of ability.
A plural noun is needed after 'their'; 'achieve' becomes 'achievement' with the suffix '-ment', then pluralised.
